pattern: Removing a target index is safe exactly when some embedding of the pattern as a subsequence never needs that character, so the true question becomes finding an embedding that uses as few of the removable positions as possible.
hints: Instead of thinking about which characters to remove, think about which characters the pattern absolutely must use to remain a subsequence -- any character not needed by some valid embedding can always be removed.

Work backward with a two-index dynamic program over (position in source, position in pattern): at each step you may skip the current source character for free, or, if it matches the current pattern character, consume it -- paying a cost of one only if that position is removable.

The dynamic program's minimum total cost is the fewest removable positions any embedding is forced to use; subtracting that from the total number of removable positions gives the maximum number of characters that can be deleted.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, only one of two overlapping removals possible
statement example, single-character pattern with two removable indices
statement example, pattern equals source, nothing removable
single-character source and pattern, the only target index
pattern needs every occurrence of a repeated letter, so no removable index can be spared
target indices are not the letters the pattern actually needs, so all can be removed
every index in source is a target index

stress.description: random lowercase source with a randomly-thinned subsequence as the pattern, half the indices removable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 500))
    source = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    pattern_chars = []
    kept = []
    for i, ch in enumerate(source):
        if rng.random() < 0.6:
            pattern_chars.append(ch)
            kept.append(i)
    if not pattern_chars:
        pattern_chars.append(source[0])
    pattern = ''.join(pattern_chars)
    targetIndices = sorted(rng.sample(range(n), max(1, n // 2)))
    return [source, pattern, targetIndices]
